Name: Jim Corbett National Park
Flora: Sal Forest, Teak, Bamboo, Haldu, Rohini
Fauna: Bengal Tiger, Asian Elephant, Leopard, Sambar Deer, Gharial
Description: India's first national park and one of the oldest tiger reserves.

Name: Kaziranga National Park
Flora: Tall Elephant Grass, Cane, Evergreen Forest
Fauna: One-Horned Rhinoceros, Bengal Tiger, Wild Buffalo, Swamp Deer
Description: Famous for conserving the Indian one-horned rhinoceros.

Name: Ranthambore National Park
Flora: Dry Deciduous Forest, Banyan, Dhonk Trees
Fauna: Royal Bengal Tiger, Leopard, Sloth Bear, Hyena
Description: One of India's best places for tiger sightings.

Name: Sundarbans National Park
Fauna: Royal Bengal Tiger, Saltwater Crocodile, Fishing Cat, Spotted Deer
Description: World's largest mangrove ecosystem and UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Name: Bandhavgarh National Park
Description: Known for one of the highest tiger densities in India.

Name: Kanha National Park
Fauna: Barasingha, Tiger, Leopard, Wild Dog, Gaur

Name: Periyar National Park
Fauna: Asian Elephant, Tiger, Sambar Deer, Nilgiri Langur
Description: Famous for its scenic lake and elephant sightings.

Name: Gir National Park
Description: The only natural habitat of the Asiatic Lion.

Name: Bandipur National Park
Description: Part of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve.

Name: Hemis National Park
Fauna: Snow Leopard, Blue Sheep, Tibetan Wolf, Himalayan Marmot
Description: India's largest national park and famous for snow leopards.
